verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Job, dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Job answered, and said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Job vastasi ja sanoi:
verses.king_james: Then Job answered and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Usquequo affligitis animam meam,  et atteritis me sermonibus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַד־אָ֭נָה תּוֹגְי֣וּן נַפְשִׁ֑י וּֽתְדַכּאוּנַ֥נִי בְמִלִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: How long do you afflict my soul, and break me in pieces with words?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuinka kauvan te vaivaatte minun sieluani? ja runtelette minua sanoilla?
verses.king_james: How long will ye vex my soul, and break me in pieces with words?

verses.latin_vulgate: En decies confunditis me,  et non erubescitis opprimentes me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זֶ֤ה עֶ֣שֶׂר פְּ֭עָמִים תַּכְלִימ֑וּנִי לֹֽא־תֵ֝בֹ֗שׁוּ תַּהְכְּרוּ־לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold, these ten times you confound me, and are not ashamed to oppress me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Te olette nyt kymmenen kertaa pilkanneet minua; ja ette häpee minua niin vaivata.
verses.king_james: These ten times have ye reproached me: ye are not ashamed that ye make yourselves strange to me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nempe etsi ignoravi,  mecum erit ignorantia mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַף־אׇמְנָ֥ם שָׁגִ֑יתִי אִ֝תִּ֗י תָּלִ֥ין מְשׁוּגָתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For if I have been ignorant, my ignorance shall be with me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minä erehdyn, niin minä itselleni erehdyn.
verses.king_james: And be it indeed that I have erred, mine error remaineth with myself.

verses.latin_vulgate: At vos contra me erigimini,  et arguitis me opprobriis meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אׇ֭מְנָם עָלַ֣י תַּגְדִּ֑ילוּ וְתוֹכִ֥יחוּ עָ֝לַ֗י חֶרְפָּתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: But you set yourselves up against me, and reprove me with my reproaches.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta te tosin nousette minua vastaan, ja soimaatte minun pilkkaani.
verses.king_james: If indeed ye will magnify yourselves against me, and plead against me my reproach:

verses.latin_vulgate: Saltem nunc intelligite quia Deus non æquo judicio afflixerit me,  et flagellis suis me cinxerit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דְּֽעוּ־אֵ֭פוֹ כִּֽי־אֱל֣וֹהַּ עִוְּתָ֑נִי וּ֝מְצוּד֗וֹ עָלַ֥י הִקִּֽיף׃
verses.douay_rheims: At least now understand, that God hath not afflicted me with an equal judgment, and compassed me with his scourges.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tietäkäät nyt, että Jumala hukuttaa minun, ja on piirittänyt minun verkkoihinsa.
verses.king_james: Know now that God hath overthrown me, and hath compassed me with his net.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce clamabo, vim patiens, et nemo audiet ;  vociferabor, et non est qui judicet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤ן אֶצְעַ֣ק חָ֭מָס וְלֹ֣א אֵעָנֶ֑ה אֲ֝שַׁוַּ֗ע וְאֵ֣ין מִשְׁפָּֽט׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold I shall cry suffering violence, and no one will hear: I shall cry aloud, and there is none to judge.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, ehkä minä vielä väkivallan tähden huutaisin, niin ei kuitenkaan kuulla minua: jos minä parkuisin, niin ei tässä ole oikeutta.
verses.king_james: Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard: I cry aloud, but there is no judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Semitam meam circumsepsit, et transire non possum :  et in calle meo tenebras posuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אׇרְחִ֣י גָ֭דַר וְלֹ֣א אֶעֱב֑וֹר וְעַ֥ל נְ֝תִיבוֹתַ֗י חֹ֣שֶׁךְ יָשִֽׂים׃
verses.douay_rheims: He hath hedged in my path round about, and I cannot pass, and in my way he hath set darkness.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on aidannat minun tieni, etten minä taida sitä käydä, ja pannut pimeyden minun poluilleni.
verses.king_james: He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.

verses.latin_vulgate: Spoliavit me gloria mea,  et abstulit coronam de capite meo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּ֭בוֹדִי מֵעָלַ֣י הִפְשִׁ֑יט וַ֝יָּ֗סַר עֲטֶ֣רֶת רֹאשִֽׁי׃
verses.douay_rheims: He hath stripped me of my glory, and hath taken the crown from my head.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on riisunut minun kunniani minulta, ja ottanut pois kruunun minun päästäni.
verses.king_james: He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Destruxit me undique, et pereo :  et quasi evulsæ arbori abstulit spem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִתְּצֵ֣נִי סָ֭בִיב וָאֵלַ֑ךְ וַיַּסַּ֥ע כָּ֝עֵ֗ץ תִּקְוָתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am lost, and he hath taken away my hope, as from a tree that is plucked up.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on maahan kukistanut minun joka kulmalta, ja laskenut minun menemään; ja on reväissyt ylös minun toivoni niinkuin puun.
verses.king_james: He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he removed like a tree.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iratus est contra me furor ejus,  et sic me habuit quasi hostem suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֣חַר עָלַ֣י אַפּ֑וֹ וַיַּחְשְׁבֵ֖נִי ל֣וֹ כְצָרָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: His wrath is kindled against me, and he hath counted me as his enemy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on hirmuisesti vihastunut minun päälleni, ja pitää minun vihamiehenänsä.
verses.king_james: He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as one of his enemies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Simul venerunt latrones ejus,  et fecerunt sibi viam per me,  et obsederunt in gyro tabernaculum meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַ֤חַד ׀ יָ֘בֹ֤אוּ גְדוּדָ֗יו וַיָּסֹ֣לּוּ עָלַ֣י דַּרְכָּ֑ם וַיַּחֲנ֖וּ סָבִ֣יב לְאׇהֳלִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: His troops have come together, and have made themselves a way by me, and have besieged my tabernacle round about.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen sotajoukkonsa ovat kokoontuneet, ja asettaneet tiensä minua kohden, ja piirittäneet minun majani.
verses.king_james: His troops come together, and raise up their way against me, and encamp round about my tabernacle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fratres meos longe fecit a me,  et noti mei quasi alieni recesserunt a me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֭חַי מֵעָלַ֣י הִרְחִ֑יק וְ֝יֹדְעַ֗י אַךְ־זָ֥רוּ מִמֶּֽנִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: He hath put my brethren far from me, and my acquaintance like strangers have departed from me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on eroittanut minun veljeni kauvas minusta, ja minun tuttavani ovat minulle muukalaisiksi tulleet.
verses.king_james: He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are verily estranged from me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dereliquerunt me propinqui mei,  et qui me noverant obliti sunt mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָדְל֥וּ קְרוֹבָ֑י וּֽמְיֻדָּעַ֥י שְׁכֵחֽוּנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: My kinsmen have forsaken me, and they that knew me, have forgotten me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun lähimmäiseni piiloivat minua, ja minun ystäväni ovat unhottaneet minun.
verses.king_james: My kinsfolk have failed, and my familiar friends have forgotten me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Inquilini domus meæ et ancillæ meæ sicut alienum habuerunt me,  et quasi peregrinus fui in oculis eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גָּ֘רֵ֤י בֵיתִ֣י וְ֭אַמְהֹתַי לְזָ֣ר תַּחְשְׁבֻ֑נִי נׇ֝כְרִ֗י הָיִ֥יתִי בְעֵינֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: They that dwell in my house, and my maidservants have counted me as a stranger, and I have been like an alien in their eyes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun huonekuntalaiseni ja piikani pitävät minua vieraana, minä olen tuntemattomaksi tullut heidän silmäinsä edessä.
verses.king_james: They that dwell in mine house, and my maids, count me for a stranger: I am an alien in their sight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servum meum vocavi, et non respondit :  ore proprio deprecabar illum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְעַבְדִּ֣י קָ֭רָאתִי וְלֹ֣א יַעֲנֶ֑ה בְּמוֹ־פִ֝֗י אֶתְחַנֶּן־לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: I called my servant, and he gave me no answer, I entreated him with my own mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä huusin palveliaani, ja ei hän vastannut minua: minun täytyy rukoilla häntä omalla suullani.
verses.king_james: I called my servant, and he gave me no answer; I intreated him with my mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Halitum meum exhorruit uxor mea,  et orabam filios uteri mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ר֭וּחִי זָ֣רָה לְאִשְׁתִּ֑י וְ֝חַנֹּתִ֗י לִבְנֵ֥י בִטְנִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: My wife hath abhorred my breath, and I entreated the children of my womb.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun emäntäni vieroi minun henkeäni, ja minun täytyy palvella omia lapsiani.
verses.king_james: My breath is strange to my wife, though I intreated for the children’s sake of mine own body.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stulti quoque despiciebant me :  et cum ab eis recessissem, detrahebant mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־עֲ֭וִילִים מָ֣אֲסוּ בִ֑י אָ֝ק֗וּמָה וַיְדַבְּרוּ־בִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Even fools despised me, and when I was gone from them, they spoke against me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja nuoretkin lapset minun katsovat ylön: jos minä nousen, niin he puhuvat minua vastaan.
verses.king_james: Yea, young children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abominati sunt me quondam consiliarii mei,  et quem maxime diligebam, aversatus est me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּ֭עֲבוּנִי כׇּל־מְתֵ֣י סוֹדִ֑י וְזֶה־אָ֝הַ֗בְתִּי נֶהְפְּכוּ־בִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: They that were sometime my counsellors, have abhorred me: and he whom I loved most is turned against me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki uskolliset ystäväni kauhistuvat minua; ja joita minä rakastin, ovat kääntäneet itsensä minua vastaan.
verses.king_james: All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loved are turned against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pelli meæ, consumptis carnibus, adhæsit os meum,  et derelicta sunt tantummodo labia circa dentes meos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּעוֹרִ֣י וּ֭בִבְשָׂרִי דָּבְקָ֣ה עַצְמִ֑י וָ֝אֶתְמַלְּטָ֗ה בְּע֣וֹר שִׁנָּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: The flesh being consumed, my bone hath cleaved to my skin, and nothing but lips are left about my teeth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun luuni tarttuivat minun nahkaani ja lihaani, ja en minä taida nahallani peittää hampaitani.
verses.king_james: My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Miseremini mei, miseremini mei saltem vos, amici mei,  quia manus Domini tetigit me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חׇנֻּ֬נִי חׇנֻּ֣נִי אַתֶּ֣ם רֵעָ֑י כִּ֥י יַד־אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ נָ֣גְעָה בִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Have pity on me, have pity on me, at least you my friends, because the hand of the Lord hath touched me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Armahtakaat minun päälleni, armahtakaat minun päälleni, te minun ystäväni! sillä Jumalan käsi on minuun sattunut!
verses.king_james: Have pity upon me, have pity upon me, O ye my friends; for the hand of God hath touched me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare persequimini me sicut Deus,  et carnibus meis saturamini ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֭מָּה תִּרְדְּפֻ֣נִי כְמוֹ־אֵ֑ל וּ֝מִבְּשָׂרִ֗י לֹ֣א תִשְׂבָּֽעוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why do you persecute me as God, and glut yourselves with my flesh?
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi te vainootte minua niinkuin Jumalakin, ja ette taida minun lihastani ravittaa?
verses.king_james: Why do ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis mihi tribuat ut scribantur sermones mei ?  quis mihi det ut exarentur in libro
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִי־יִתֵּ֣ן אֵ֭פוֹ וְיִכָּתְב֣וּן מִלָּ֑י מִי־יִתֵּ֖ן בַּסֵּ֣פֶר וְיֻחָֽקוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who will grant me that my words may be written? who will grant me that they may be marked down in a book?
verses.finnish_biblia: Jospa minun puheeni kirjoitettaisiin! jospa ne kirjaan pantaisiin, ja painettaisiin!
verses.king_james: Oh that my words were now written! oh that they were printed in a book!

verses.latin_vulgate: stylo ferreo et plumbi lamina,  vel celte sculpantur in silice ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּעֵט־בַּרְזֶ֥ל וְעֹפָ֑רֶת לָ֝עַ֗ד בַּצּ֥וּר יֵחָצְבֽוּן׃
verses.douay_rheims: With an iron pen and in a plate of lead, or else be graven with an instrument in flint stone?
verses.finnish_biblia: Raudalla kaivettaisiin lyijy ijankaikkiseksi muistoksi kiveen.
verses.king_james: That they were graven with an iron pen and lead in the rock for ever!

verses.latin_vulgate: Scio enim quod redemptor meus vivit,  et in novissimo die de terra surrecturus sum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֣י יָ֭דַעְתִּי גֹּ֣אֲלִי חָ֑י וְ֝אַחֲר֗וֹן עַל־עָפָ֥ר יָקֽוּם׃
verses.douay_rheims: For I know that my Redeemer liveth, and in the last day I shall rise out of the earth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä minä tiedän minun Lunastajani elävän: ja hän on tästälähin maan päällä seisova.
verses.king_james: For I know that my redeemer liveth, and that he shall stand at the latter day upon the earth:

verses.latin_vulgate: et rursum circumdabor pelle mea,  et in carne mea videbo Deum meum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַחַ֣ר ע֭וֹרִי נִקְּפוּ־זֹ֑את וּ֝מִבְּשָׂרִ֗י אֶחֱזֶ֥ה אֱלֽוֹהַּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I shall be clothed again with my skin, and in my flesh I shall see my God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja vaikka vihdoin minun nahkani ja tämä (ruumis) lakastuu, saan minä kuitenkin minun lihassani nähdä Jumalan.
verses.king_james: And though after my skin worms destroy this body, yet in my flesh shall I see God:

verses.latin_vulgate: quem visurus sum ego ipse,  et oculi mei conspecturi sunt, et non alius :  reposita est hæc spes mea in sinu meo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֤ר אֲנִ֨י ׀ אֶחֱזֶה־לִּ֗י וְעֵינַ֣י רָא֣וּ וְלֹא־זָ֑ר כָּל֖וּ כִלְיֹתַ֣י בְּחֵקִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whom I myself shall see, and my eyes shall behold, and not another: this my hope is laid up in my bosom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen minä olen minulleni näkevä, ja minun silmäni katsovat häntä, ja ei kenkään outo. Minun munaskuuni ovat kuluneet minun helmassani.
verses.king_james: Whom I shall see for myself, and mine eyes shall behold, and not another; though my reins be consumed within me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare ergo nunc dicitis : Persequamur eum,  et radicem verbi inveniamus contra eum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י תֹ֭אמְרוּ מַה־נִּרְדׇּף־ל֑וֹ וְשֹׁ֥רֶשׁ דָּ֝בָ֗ר נִמְצָא־בִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why then do you say now: Let us persecute him, and let us find occasion of word against him?
verses.finnish_biblia: Tosin teidän pitäis sanoman: Miksi me vainoamme häntä? sillä tämän puheen perustus löytyy minun tykönäni.
verses.king_james: But ye should say, Why persecute we him, seeing the root of the matter is found in me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Fugite ergo a facie gladii,  quoniam ultor iniquitatum gladius est :  et scitote esse judicium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גּ֤וּרוּ לָכֶ֨ם ׀ מִפְּנֵי־חֶ֗רֶב כִּֽי־חֵ֭מָה עֲוֺנ֣וֹת חָ֑רֶב לְמַ֖עַן תֵּדְע֣וּן (שדין) [שַׁדּֽוּן]׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Flee then from the face of the sword, for the sword is the revenger of iniquities: and know ye that there is a judgment.
verses.finnish_biblia: Peljätkäät siis miekkaa, sillä viha on pahain töiden kostomiekka, että te tietäisitte kurituksen tulevan.
verses.king_james: Be ye afraid of the sword: for wrath bringeth the punishments of the sword, that ye may know there is a judgment.
